sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The board still didn't change. Let me reconsider the problem. Looking at the image more carefully:
The board has 4 regions:
1. Top-left: 3x3 grid of blue/red blocks with a special center
2. Top-right: 3x3 grid of blue/red blocks with a special center
3. Bottom-left: 3x3 grid of red/blue blocks with a special center  
4. Bottom-right: 3x3 grid of blue blocks inside a grey frame with a special center
The orange bar at the bottom (id=64, 64 pixels) might be a timer or progress bar.
Let me look at the special centers more carefully. They seem to have small 2x2 white cells with grey and red sub-elements. These might be the interactive elements.
Let me try clicking on the actual colored blocks (the 6x6 pixel blocks) rather than the center elements. Or maybe I need to click on the grey L-shaped frame pieces.
Actually, wait - let me re-examine. The grey pieces (id=33, 35, 62, 63) are L-shaped corner pieces forming a frame around the bottom-right grid. Maybe I need to click on these grey corner pieces to "move" them or something.
Let me try clicking on different parts of the board systematically. Let me try clicking on one of the blue blocks in the top-left grid.

<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# Top-left grid: blue block at center (4,6) - let me click there
# Also let me look at the orange bar at bottom

# Try clicking on a blue block in top-left grid
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 4, 'col': 6}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
